# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
"""Optimized circle packing for n=26 circles with greedy radius expansion."""
import numpy as np


def construct_packing():
    """
    Construct an optimized arrangement of 26 circles using:
    1. Strategic corner placement for larger circles
    2. Hexagonal interior pattern
    3. Greedy radius expansion instead of shrink-only
    
    Returns:
        Tuple of (centers, radii, sum_of_radii)
    """
    n = 26
    centers = np.zeros((n, 2))
    
    # Place 4 corner circles - can be quite large (radius ~0.12)
    r_corner = 0.12
    centers[0] = [r_corner, r_corner]
    centers[1] = [1-r_corner, r_corner]
    centers[2] = [r_corner, 1-r_corner]
    centers[3] = [1-r_corner, 1-r_corner]
    
    # Place 4 edge circles per side (between corners) = 16 edge circles
    # Bottom edge (y ~ 0.1)
    for i in range(4):
        centers[4+i] = [0.18 + 0.22*i, 0.1]
    # Top edge (y ~ 0.9)
    for i in range(4):
        centers[8+i] = [0.18 + 0.22*i, 0.9]
    # Left edge (x ~ 0.1)
    for i in range(4):
        centers[12+i] = [0.1, 0.18 + 0.22*i]
    # Right edge (x ~ 0.9)
    for i in range(4):
        centers[16+i] = [0.9, 0.18 + 0.22*i]
    
    # Place 6 interior circles in 2 rows of 3
    for i in range(3):
        centers[20+i] = [0.3 + 0.2*i, 0.35]
        centers[23+i] = [0.3 + 0.2*i, 0.65]
    
    # Compute optimal radii with greedy expansion
    radii = compute_max_radii_greedy(centers)
    return centers, radii, np.sum(radii)


def compute_max_radii_greedy(centers):
    """Compute maximum radii using greedy expansion with boundary constraints."""
    n = centers.shape[0]
    radii = np.zeros(n)
    
    # Initialize each radius to min distance to boundary
    for i in range(n):
        radii[i] = min(centers[i,0], centers[i,1], 1-centers[i,0], 1-centers[i,1])
    
    # Greedy expansion: try to grow each circle
    for _ in range(200):
        for i in range(n):
            # Find max radius for circle i given current state
            max_r = min(centers[i,0], centers[i,1], 1-centers[i,0], 1-centers[i,1])
            for j in range(n):
                if i != j:
                    dist = np.linalg.norm(centers[i] - centers[j])
                    max_r = min(max_r, dist - radii[j])
            radii[i] = max(0, max_r)
    
    return radii


# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END


# This part remains fixed (not evolved)
def run_packing():
    """Run the circle packing constructor for n=26"""
    centers, radii, sum_radii = construct_packing()
    return centers, radii, sum_radii


def visualize(centers, radii):
    """
    Visualize the circle packing

    Args:
        centers: np.array of shape (n, 2) with (x, y) coordinates
        radii: np.array of shape (n) with radius of each circle
    """
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    from matplotlib.patches import Circle

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))

    # Draw unit square
    ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
    ax.set_aspect("equal")
    ax.grid(True)

    # Draw circles
    for i, (center, radius) in enumerate(zip(centers, radii)):
        circle = Circle(center, radius, alpha=0.5)
        ax.add_patch(circle)
        ax.text(center[0], center[1], str(i), ha="center", va="center")

    plt.title(f"Circle Packing (n={len(centers)}, sum={sum(radii):.6f})")
    plt.show()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    centers, radii, sum_radii = run_packing()
    print(f"Sum of radii: {sum_radii}")
    # AlphaEvolve improved this to 2.635

    # Uncomment to visualize:
    visualize(centers, radii)
